qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated remote code execution via AUTO_LOGIN bypass and exec() in validate_code endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes an IDOR in the flow API (_read_flow in flows.py) where a missing ownership check lets authenticated users access other users' flows. This finding describes RCE via exec() in the validate_code endpoint (validate.py). The root cause, vulnerable code path, and vulnerability class are entirely different: the advisory is CWE-639/CWE-862 (authorization bypass/missing authorization) in the flow CRUD operations, while this finding is about unsafe code execution in a code-validation endpoint.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes an IDOR in Langflow's _read_flow helper (src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/flows.py) where a missing ownership check allows authenticated users to read, modify, or delete any flow regardless of owner. Finding auto_login_rce_validate_code describes RCE via exec() in the validate_code endpoint — a different vulnerable code path, different root cause (unsafe code execution vs missing authorization), and different vulnerability class.
evidence: Advisory root cause: _read_flow in flows.py branches on AUTO_LOGIN and omits Flow.user_id == user_id filter when AUTO_LOGIN is False, allowing any authenticated user to access any flow.

Finding auto_login_rce_validate_code root cause: exec() in validate_code (validate.py:57) evaluates default-argument expressions in user-supplied function definitions. This is a code-injection vulnerability, not an authorization-bypass IDOR.
